text: Commute
Congestion
A detour
A roundabout
Rush hour
A one-way street
To hail a cab
Gridlock
A crosswalk
Public transit
A shortcut
A pedestrian
To pull over
Could you pull over at the next crosswalk?
Take the street that leads to the station.
The driver who picked us up knew a shortcut.
The old bridge, which closes at rush hour, is faster.
That is the square where the market is held.
Hello, could you take me to the Belmonte Hotel, which is near the old market square?
Of course. The road that usually goes there is closed for repairs, so we will take a small detour. Don t worry, I know a shortcut.
Thank you. Is there much congestion at this time? The driver who brought me yesterday got stuck for ages.
It is rush hour, so the centre, which is always busy, can be gridlock. But my route avoids the big roundabout where everyone gets stuck.
Perfect. Could you pull over at the crosswalk near the hotel? I d rather walk the last bit, which gives me a feel for the area.
Good idea. Just remember it is a one-way street, so walk against the traffic. Here we are, that is the lane that leads straight to the square.

Right, hop in. Now, the road that usually takes you downtown is completely closed for repairs, so we will have to take a detour. Don't worry -- I know a shortcut, which most tourists never find. We will avoid the worst of the congestion near the river, where the traffic at rush hour is honestly a nightmare. There is a roundabout coming up; the third exit, which is the one we want, leads straight to your hotel. If you ever come back, my advice is simple: never drive yourself in this city. Use public transit, or hail a cab like a local. It is faster, cheaper, and far less stressful.

I used to be terrified of getting around in a city I didn't know. These days, I actually enjoy it. The trick, which took me years to learn, is to slow down and read the place. I look for the crosswalks where locals gather, the one-way streets that only buses use, and the little shortcuts that never appear on a map. My favourite moment is rush hour, when the whole city is in motion -- the commuters who rush past, the cyclists who weave through the gridlock. A new city, which once felt like a maze, becomes a puzzle I love to solve. That, for me, is the real joy of travel.
